title: astragalus uttaranchalensis
intro: Astragalus uttaranchalensis, also known as the Uttaranchal milkvetch or milk vetch, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Himalayan region and has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is known for its immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving cough and cold, and improving overall immune function. Astragalus uttaranchalensis is often used in herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and powder, and is typically consumed in the form of capsules or tablets.
